headlines.title: Colorado primary election results: Melat Kiros wins – key takeaways
headlines.snippet: Melat Kiros has won the Colorado primary election, defeating Diana DeGette. DeGette had served in office for as long as the 29-year-old Kiros has been alive.

headlines.title: Algeria heads to legislative polls amid record-low turnout fear
headlines.snippet: Algeria is preparing for legislative elections amid fears of record-low voter turnout. Factors contributing to this concern include post-Hirak distrust, the exclusion of candidates, and general political apathy.

headlines.title: Man charged after arson attack on Islamic centre in Dublin
headlines.snippet: A man has been charged in connection with an arson attack on an Islamic centre and mosque in Dublin. During the incident, the man reportedly shouted Islamophobic and anti-immigrant slogans.

headlines.title: UN Human Rights Council to hold urgent meeting on Sudan crisis
headlines.snippet: The UN Human Rights Council will convene an urgent meeting to address the escalating crisis in Sudan. This emergency session comes after warnings that approximately 500,000 civilians near el-Obeid are facing the threat of large-scale atrocities.

headlines.title: US Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship: Who wins, who loses?
headlines.snippet: The US Supreme Court has upheld birthright citizenship, striking down an executive order by US President Trump that sought to abolish it. Trump has stated his intention to challenge this ruling.

headlines.title: Exclusive: How British Museum made false claims about its removal of ‘Palestine’ from displays
headlines.snippet: An investigation by Middle East Eye reveals that the British Museum made false claims regarding its removal of the term 'Palestine' from displays. Internal emails indicate that these changes stemmed directly from complaints by pro-Israel activists. The museum and its director subsequently misled critics.

headlines.title: Palestine Action case reveals the limits of terrorism discourse
headlines.snippet: The Palestine Action case is highlighting the complexities and limitations of terrorism discourse. Court proceedings are focusing on flawed assumptions that link terrorism to a racialized image of the Muslim threat. Protests in London supported Palestine Action, with demonstrators holding signs against genocide and the arming of Israel.

headlines.title: Israel is carrying out 'reproductive genocide' against Palestinians
headlines.snippet: A new report by the Palestinian Feminist Collective alleges that Israel has been carrying out 'reproductive genocide' against Palestinians for decades. The report claims this involves the obliteration of medical institutions, execution of women and children, and environmental degradation leading to infertility, accelerating since October 2023.

headlines.title: Israeli drone strike targets civilians in Gaza City
headlines.snippet: An Israeli drone strike in Gaza City has wounded several Palestinians, with some reported to be in critical condition. The attack targeted a group of civilians near Dar Al-Arqam School in the Sheikh Radwan neighborhood, according to Wafa news agency.

headlines.title: Israel arrests five women activists in the occupied West Bank
headlines.snippet: Israeli forces have arrested five women activists in the occupied West Bank. A prisoners' group indicated that arrests of female activists by Israeli forces have recently escalated.

headlines.title: Iran warns of immediate response to any threat, cites US commitment to restrain Israel
headlines.snippet: Iran has issued a warning, stating it will respond immediately to any threat. The country cited a US commitment to restrain Israel as part of its statement.

headlines.title: US citizenship: Entitled birthright or outdated privilege? Supreme Court overrules Trump
headlines.snippet: The US Supreme Court has overruled Trump, impacting the discussion around birthright citizenship. This ruling determines whether it is an entitled right or an outdated privilege.

headlines.title: ‘Releasing frozen Iranian funds politically difficult for Trump’: Former US Envoy
headlines.snippet: A former US Envoy suggests that releasing frozen Iranian funds would be politically difficult for Trump. The statement indicates a complex situation regarding financial matters.

headlines.title: Can Iran Play the Long Game?
headlines.snippet: The article 'Can Iran Play the Long Game?' explores strategic possibilities for Iran. It is published by Al Arabiya English.

headlines.title: Vessel approached by multiple small craft with armed people off Yemen, UKMTO says
headlines.snippet: The UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) reported that a vessel was approached by multiple small craft carrying armed individuals off the coast of Yemen.

headlines.title: US-Saudi relations 'strained' after Iran operation dispute
headlines.snippet: Saudi and US relations are reportedly strained following a dispute over the kingdom's initial refusal to allow Washington to use its bases for an operation against Iran.

headlines.title: Retrofitted Qatari jet takes maiden flight as Air Force One
headlines.snippet: A refurbished Qatari jet, painted in Trump’s preferred colors, has taken its maiden flight, serving as Air Force One.

headlines.title: Photo of bound, blindfolded Palestinian sparks outrage
headlines.snippet: The kidnapping of a Palestinian has been attributed to Israel’s Netzah Yehuda battalion, which is known for alleged inhumane conduct towards Palestinians. A photo of the bound and blindfolded individual has sparked outrage.

headlines.title: 120 dead in latest Sudan cholera outbreak: WHO
headlines.snippet: The World Health Organization (WHO) reported that 120 deaths have occurred in the latest cholera outbreak in Sudan. Between July 2024 and March 2026, the previous wave saw over 124,400 infections and 3,500 fatalities.

headlines.title: Sudan's El Obeid faces 'El Fasher repeat' amid RSF drone strikes
headlines.snippet: Sudan's El Obeid is facing a situation described as a potential 'El Fasher repeat' due to drone strikes by the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). The city has become a critical location in the conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the RSF.

headlines.title: إيران: أمين المجلس الأعلى للأمن القومي الإيراني: قضية الثأر لشهداء إيران مفتوحة وسيُعاقب مرتكبو الجرائم في الوقت المناسب
headlines.snippet: Iran's Supreme National Security Council Secretary stated that the case for avenging Iranian martyrs is open and that perpetrators will be punished in due time.

headlines.title: إيران: التلفزيون الإيراني: جنوح سفينة أجنبية في مضيق هرمز كانت تسلك مساراً غير المسار المحدد وفق التعليمات الإيرانية
headlines.snippet: Iranian state television reported that a foreign ship ran aground in the Strait of Hormuz after deviating from its designated course and ignoring Iranian instructions.

headlines.title: "معاريف": "اتفاق الإطار" مع لبنان حبر على ورق.. وفرص تطبيقه ضئيلة
headlines.snippet: The newspaper 'Maariv' suggests that the 'framework agreement' with Lebanon is merely symbolic, with slim chances of implementation.

headlines.title: Iran warns Israel: Any threat against Leader, people will meet firm response
headlines.snippet: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi warned that any threat against Iran's Leader or people would be met with an immediate and powerful response. This statement was in reaction to remarks made by the Israeli war minister.

headlines.title: Funeral ceremonies of martyred Leader should symbolize national unity and cohesion: President
headlines.snippet: President Masoud Pezeshkian called for national unity and cohesion during the farewell and burial ceremonies for the martyred Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei, and some family members.

headlines.title: Iran’s top negotiator meets with Qatari PM as working groups for final deal established in Doha
headlines.snippet: Iran's senior nuclear negotiator, Kazem Gharibabadi, announced that working groups have been formed in Doha to follow up on an understanding and negotiate a final agreement.

headlines.title: Ghalibaf praises national team: You fought with full strength and displayed Iran’s unity, return home with pride
headlines.snippet: The speaker of Iran’s Parliament praised the national football team for their performance in the 2026 FIFA World Cup, highlighting their strength and display of national unity.

headlines.title: Those responsible for Leader’s martyrdom will face justice: Security chief
headlines.snippet: The Secretary of Iran’s Supreme National Security Council, Mohammad Baqer Zolqadr, stated that those responsible for the assassination of the country's Leader and other martyrs will face justice.

headlines.title: Talks btw Iran, US for a final agreement not yet started
headlines.snippet: Negotiations between Iran and the US for a final agreement have not yet commenced, according to Mehr News Agency.

headlines.title: Any threat against Iranians to receive immediate response
headlines.snippet: Any threat against Iranians will receive an immediate response, as reported by Mehr News Agency.

headlines.title: Iran’s missile, UAV capabilities nonnegotiable: Defense min.
headlines.snippet: Iran's Defense Minister stated that the country's missile and UAV capabilities are non-negotiable.

headlines.title: FM Araghchi lashes out at US Sec.’s remarks over Iran at WC
headlines.snippet: Foreign Minister Araghchi criticized remarks made by the US Secretary concerning Iran at the World Cup.

headlines.title: Israeli forces blow up homes in three south Lebanon villages
headlines.snippet: Israeli forces have blown up homes in three villages located in southern Lebanon.

headlines.title: Iranians barely visible in US-Iran deal, UN rapporteur says - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: A UN rapporteur indicated that Iranians have minimal visibility in the US-Iran deal, as reported by Iran International.

headlines.title: IRGC moves to seize historic Protestant church in Tehran - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: The IRGC is reportedly moving to seize a historic Protestant church in Tehran, according to Iran International.

headlines.snippet: The article 'Atlas of Iran’s Missile Cities' is a publication from Iran International.

headlines.title: Khamenei to end Eje'i’s judiciary tenure after one term
headlines.snippet: Khamenei plans to end Eje'i’s tenure as head of the judiciary after one term, as reported by Iran International.

headlines.title: Deadly attacks shake northwest Iran as IRGC reports new clashes - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: Deadly attacks have occurred in northwestern Iran, with the IRGC reporting new clashes, according to Iran International.

headlines.title: World Bank approves $700 million in financing to boost investment, jobs in Jordan
headlines.snippet: The World Bank has approved $700 million in financing to support investment and job creation in Jordan.

headlines.title: In south Lebanon, Netanyahu says Israel will stay as long as Hizbollah 'threatens us'
headlines.snippet: Netanyahu stated that Israel will remain in Lebanon as long as Hezbollah poses a threat, according to the Jordan Times.

headlines.title: Thousands expected as Vespa celebrates 80 years in Rome
headlines.snippet: Thousands of people are expected to gather in Rome to celebrate Vespa's 80th anniversary.

headlines.title: RJ following up on crew bus collision in New York
headlines.snippet: RJ is following up on a crew bus collision that occurred in New York.

headlines.title: GDP rises to 2.9% in Q1 2026, up from 2.7% in 2025 — DoS
headlines.snippet: The Gross Domestic Product (GDP) rose to 2.9% in Q1 2026, an increase from 2.7% in 2025, according to the Department of Statistics.

headlines.title: Katz: Katz: Israel will remain in Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza ‘indefinitely'; Israel establishes 2 new military checkpoints near Naqoura, southern Lebanon
headlines.snippet: Katz stated that Israel will remain in Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza indefinitely. Israel has also established two new military checkpoints near Naqoura in southern Lebanon.

headlines.title: House of Representatives rejects resolution calling for 'withdrawal of US armed forces from all hostilities in Lebanon'
headlines.snippet: The House of Representatives rejected a resolution that called for the withdrawal of US armed forces from all hostilities in Lebanon.

headlines.title: Indirect Iran-US technical talks underway in Doha, source says
headlines.snippet: Indirect technical talks between Iran and the US are currently underway in Doha, according to a source.

headlines.title: Passengers delighted to ride UAE's first ever passenger train
headlines.snippet: Passengers have expressed delight riding the UAE's first passenger train.

headlines.title: Dangerous heat wave to grip US as nation marks 250 years
headlines.snippet: A dangerous heat wave is expected to affect the US as the nation prepares to mark its 250th anniversary.

headlines.title: White House lifts export control on Anthropic that froze its most advanced models
headlines.snippet: The US government has lifted export controls on Anthropic's most advanced AI models, including Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5. The company stated it would begin restoring access soon.

headlines.title: Oman has delivered a proposal on the future of the Strait of Hormuz to the US, sources say
headlines.snippet: Oman has submitted a proposal to the US and its allies regarding the future of the Strait of Hormuz. The proposal outlines a plan for shipping companies to pay service fees for passage.

headlines.title: The Strait of Hormuz pain has been China’s gain, The Asia Group says
headlines.snippet: The strategic advisory firm The Asia Group reports that China has benefited the most from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The firm noted that the disruption of oil and commodity shipments has unevenly impacted Asian economies.

headlines.title: US, Iranian officials hold indirect, lower-level talks in Doha, source says
headlines.snippet: US and Iranian officials are engaged in indirect, lower-level technical talks in Doha, facilitated by Qatari and Pakistani mediators. US special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner met with Qatar's prime minister in Doha.

headlines.title: Egypt’s Former Irrigation Minister warns not to underestimate impact of GERD
headlines.snippet: Former Egyptian Minister of Water Resources and Irrigation, Mohamed Nasr-Eddin Allam, cautioned against underestimating the impact of the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam (GERD) on Egypt and Sudan.
